settings.editor: Home > About

Stuvia is a small studio company with a simple belief: great creative work deserves a great room to be made in. We design, build, and host premium spaces so creators, brands and podcasters can show up and focus on the work.
Studio types
Creators booked
Hours produced
Average rating
Five years ago, our founder was recording a podcast in a noisy co-working space, lugging gear up three flights of stairs and apologizing to guests about the lighting. There had to be a better way.

Stuvia was built around that frustration — a place where the gear is already racked, the room is already tuned, and a real person is on hand to help. No deposits, no week-long onboarding, no awkward keys-under-the-mat handoff.
Today our studios host indie creators, Fortune 500 brands, agencies and first-time podcasters. The thing they share is a love for craft — and a refusal to let logistics get in the way of it.

settings.sg_icon_description: Every space is curated end-to-end — from acoustics to lighting to the chair you sit in. No wasted detail.
We build for people who actually shoot, record, and create. Not for catalogs or stock photos.
A real human host greets you, walks you through the gear, and stays one message away the whole shoot.
Transparent rates by the hour, half-day or full-day. No deposits, no junk fees, no surprises.

settings.editor: A short timeline of how we got here — and what's next.
Stuvia starts as a single podcast room rented out on weekends.
Two new studios open — daylight photo and a cinema stage.
We hire full-time studio hosts so creators are never alone.
The flagship location opens with six bookable creative spaces.
We're producers, engineers and hosts who would rather be on set than in a meeting. You'll meet most of us when you book.
